text.name: system_description
text.text: The Compact Disc-Interactive (CD-i) is a digital optical disc data storage format as well as a hardware platform, co-developed and marketed by Dutch company Philips and Japanese company Sony. The first CD-i system was produced by Philips in collaboration with Kyocera in 1988. By 1994, sales of CD-i systems had begun to slow, and in 1998 the product line was dropped.
